name: Basic Swedish Meatballs
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat milk until warm; do not boil.
2. In a separate bowl, combine ground beef, grated onion, nutmeg, ginger, salt, and pepper.
3. Gradually add flour to the mixture, stirring thoroughly to ensure even distribution.
4. Mix in the beaten eggs until the mixture is light and puffy, about 5 minutes.
5. Cover and refrigerate for 1/2 hour.
6. Roll the beef mixture into 1-inch balls.
7. Brown the meatballs under the broiler.
8. Remove from heat and add flour to the drippings from the meatballs.
9. Stir until smooth, then gradually stir in the beef stock and dry sherry.
10. Bring to a boil and season with salt and pepper.
11. Simmer for 5 minutes.
12. Add the meatballs to the sauce and simmer gently covered for an additional 15 minutes.
13. Place in serving dish.
